questions.question: Your team needs to ensure all AI-generated FastAPI code uses async handlers and Pydantic v2. What's the most appropriate approach?
options: Fine-tune the model on your codebase so it learns your conventions
Build a RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant code examples from your repo
Create a skill file with the conventions and anti-patterns — it loads before code generation tasks
Add the rules to every user message as a prefix
questions.explanation: This is a small, curated set of team conventions — exactly what skills are designed for. Fine-tuning is overkill and slow to iterate. RAG is for large corpora, not a few hundred words of rules. Prefixing every message wastes tokens and is error-prone. A skill file is versioned, deterministic, and loads automatically.

questions.question: What is the fundamental difference between how skills and RAG provide context to the model?
options: Skills use embeddings to find relevant content; RAG uses keyword matching
Skills load a specific file deterministically by path; RAG uses semantic search to find relevant chunks probabilistically
Skills are injected at training time; RAG is injected at inference time
Skills work only with Claude; RAG works with any model
questions.explanation: Skills are deterministic file reads — same task type, same file, same content every time. RAG uses embeddings and vector search to find semantically relevant chunks, which is probabilistic (results vary by query). This distinction matters: skills are predictable but don't scale; RAG scales but introduces retrieval uncertainty.

questions.question:
This skill file is poorly structured. What's the most impactful problem?

```markdown
# Everything About Python

Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language created by Guido van Rossum.
It supports multiple paradigms including procedural, object-oriented, and functional.
Python uses dynamic typing and garbage collection. The standard library includes
modules for file I/O, networking, regular expressions, unit testing, and more.

## Data Types
Python has several built-in data types: int, float, str, list, dict, tuple, set...
[continues for 4,000 more words covering all of Python]
```
options: It should use JSON instead of markdown
It's too broad and too large — not scoped to a specific task, and at ~5,000 words it wastes ~6,500 tokens of context budget on generic knowledge the model already has
The heading structure is wrong — it should use H2 not H1
questions.explanation: Two problems compound here: (1) No task scoping — 'Everything About Python' loads for every Python task, even when irrelevant. (2) Massive context cost — ~6,500 tokens of generic knowledge the model already knows from training. Good skills are narrow (scoped to a specific task/library), prescriptive (rules, not tutorials), and concise (<500 words).

questions.question: Agent output quality degrades noticeably as conversations exceed 50K tokens. You have two 2,000-word skill files loading on every request. What's the most likely root cause?
options: The skill files contain syntax errors that confuse the model
Skills only work in short conversations
The skills consume ~5,000 tokens per request, and as conversation history grows, the combined context pressure reduces the model's effective reasoning space
The model caches skill content and it becomes stale over time
questions.explanation: Context window is a fixed resource. Skills (~5K tokens) + long conversation history + tool results all compete for space. As the conversation grows, the remaining space for the model to reason shrinks. Fix: trim skills to essentials, split into task-specific sub-skills so only one loads per task, or summarize conversation history.
